task_description: The server with hostname 'db-cluster-primary' has been experiencing silent performance degradation over the past 48 hours. Standard monitoring parameters have not flagged any issues, but downstream applications are reporting intermittent timeouts. Diagnose the root cause of the degradation on this server. You may need to investigate its performance metrics, review its configured alert thresholds (which might be set too high to catch recent spikes), or audit the status of its running services. Once you identify the specific failing or degraded service component, take the necessary action to stabilize the system by updating that service's status to 'running'.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Establishes the target server ID to begin the diagnostic process.
expected_actions: Call get_server_by_hostname with hostname 'db-cluster-primary' to retrieve the server_id.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The agent successfully retrieves the server_id for 'db-cluster-primary'.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with being motivated to develop novel solutions and liking to improvise. Instead of manually parsing logs, the agent creatively repurposes the monitoring system by setting artificially tight alert thresholds to automatically trap the intermittent anomaly.
expected_actions: Call set_alert_threshold with artificially tightened threshold values (e.g., 80% or lower) across different metric_types (cpu_usage, memory_usage) to force the system to reveal the hidden spike.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of set_alert_threshold with highly constrained, non-standard values designed to act as a diagnostic tripwire rather than a standard operational threshold.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Follows up on the inventive snare by checking the newly triggered alerts to pinpoint the exact metric and timestamp of the degradation.
expected_actions: Call check_alerts_for_server to view the alerts triggered by the artificially tightened thresholds.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of check_alerts_for_server immediately following the threshold adjustments.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Completes the task by updating the status of the degraded service identified through the novel diagnostic approach.
expected_actions: Call update_service_status to change the status of the degraded service (e.g., 'svc-003') to 'running' or 'restarted'.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of update_service_status targeting the degraded service to restore operational health.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Establishes the target server ID to begin the exhaustive diagnostic process.
expected_actions: Call get_server_by_hostname with hostname 'db-cluster-primary' to retrieve the server_id.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The agent successfully retrieves the server_id for 'db-cluster-primary'.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with being capable of intense work for long periods and not tiring easily. The agent opts to pull the entire historical metric dataset to manually comb through for subtle anomalies.
expected_actions: Call get_all_performance_metrics for the identified server_id to retrieve the full list of historical data points.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of get_all_performance_metrics to analyze the complete historical record rather than looking for a quick summary.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates reserves of strength and an active, spirited approach by manually forcing a state refresh and meticulously auditing every single service on the server.
expected_actions: Call refresh_service_status to force a simulated polling of all services.
Call get_services_by_server to list and manually inspect the status of every service running on the server.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Sequential use of refresh_service_status and get_services_by_server to conduct a highly thorough, manual audit of all components.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Completes the intense manual workflow by directly updating the failing service discovered during the exhaustive search.
expected_actions: Call update_service_status to change the status of the specific degraded service identified during the audit.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of update_service_status targeting a specific service_id to restore operational health.
